exercises.title: Hello World
Variables
exercises.description: Declare int age = 20 and double price = 99.99. Print the price.

exercises.title: Strings
exercises.description: Create a string greeting = "Hello, Sanji!" and print it.

exercises.title: If-Else
exercises.description: Given int x = 20, print Greater than 10 if true, else 10 or less.

exercises.title: For Loop
exercises.description: Print Iteration: 0 through Iteration: 4 using a for loop.
exercises.hint: Use for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++) { cout << "Iteration: " << i << "\n"; }

exercises.title: Functions
exercises.description: Create a void greet() function that prints Hello from inside a function!. Call it.
exercises.hint: void greet() { cout << "Hello from inside a function!" << endl; }

exercises.title: Arrays
exercises.description: Declare int nums[5] = {10, 20, 30, 40, 50} and print the first element.

exercises.title: Classes & Objects
exercises.description: Create a Car class with a public string brand. Set it to Toyota and print it.
exercises.hint: class Car { public: string brand; }; Car c; c.brand = "Toyota";

exercises.title: Inheritance
exercises.description: Create a Vehicle class with honk() that prints Beep beep!. Create Car inheriting from Vehicle. Call honk().
exercises.hint: class Vehicle { public: void honk() { cout << "Beep beep!\n"; } }; class Car : public Vehicle {};

exercises.title: Vectors
exercises.description: Create a vector<int> with {1,2,3}, push_back 4 and 5, then print all elements.
exercises.hint: Use push_back(4); push_back(5); and a range-based for loop.
